jokes.text: Well, don't blame me. It took Dee a goddamn hour to get dressed like a man.
jokes.setup: The gang is late and Dennis is deflecting blame
jokes.punchline: Reveals Dee had to dress as a man for some reason, treating it as a normal delay
jokes.explanation: Good character-specific humor that establishes the absurd normalcy of Dee's bizarre behavior

dialogue.speaker: Dennis
dialogue.line: Well, don't blame me. It took Dee a goddamn hour to get dressed like a man.

jokes.text: Captain Barnacle. It's a great new character I'm working on. Argh!
jokes.setup: Charlie mentions a character name out of nowhere
jokes.punchline: It's just a generic pirate character with the most basic pirate exclamation
jokes.explanation: Perfect Charlie randomness - he thinks this basic pirate impression is a great character

dialogue.speaker: Charlie
dialogue.line: Captain Barnacle. It's a great new character I'm working on. Argh!

jokes.text: Well, we kind of soaked them in alcohol and you suck the alcohol off of them.
jokes.setup: Someone asks about bringing worms to what should be a classy event
jokes.punchline: Charlie matter-of-factly explains the disgusting process of alcohol-soaked worms
jokes.explanation: Classic Charlie gross-out humor delivered with complete sincerity

dialogue.speaker: Charlie
dialogue.line: Well, we kind of soaked them in alcohol and you suck the alcohol off of them.

jokes.text: I ate mine.
jokes.setup: Charlie explains the alcohol-worm process
jokes.punchline: Casually reveals he just ate his worm instead of following his own instructions
jokes.explanation: Perfect economy - three words that completely undercut his explanation and show Charlie's impulsiveness

dialogue.speaker: Charlie
dialogue.line: I ate mine.

jokes.text: Look, guys, this car was built for this, okay? It's got air intake valves. This is precision British land-to-sea craftsmanship at work.
jokes.setup: Dennis wants to drive into water to catch the boat
jokes.punchline: Delivers completely fake technical justification with complete confidence
jokes.explanation: Perfect Dennis delusion - inventing technical specs to justify his insane plan

dialogue.speaker: Dennis
dialogue.line: Look, guys, this car was built for this, okay? It's got air intake valves. This is precision British land-to-sea craftsmanship at work.

jokes.text: I'm a chauffeur to a bunch of worm-sucking idiots!
jokes.setup: Dennis is lamenting what they've become
jokes.punchline: Reduces his friends to this specific degrading description
jokes.explanation: Great callback that creates a memorable insult specific to this episode's events

dialogue.speaker: Dennis
dialogue.line: I'm a chauffeur to a bunch of worm-sucking idiots!

jokes.text: I used to be a party boy who banged chicks all the time. You guys remember?
jokes.setup: Mac is also lamenting what he's become
jokes.punchline: His pathetic plea for validation about his past sexual prowess
jokes.explanation: Classic Mac insecurity masked as bragging, the 'You guys remember?' makes it pathetic

dialogue.speaker: Mac
dialogue.line: I used to be a party boy who banged chicks all the time. You guys remember?

jokes.text: I mean, what, what if I said I wanted to become a man cheetah right now, what would you do? / I'd go get the spots. / He's got spots!
jokes.setup: Dennis uses a hypothetical to show how absurd they've become
jokes.punchline: Frank immediately offers to enable it and Mac reveals spots already exist
jokes.explanation: Brilliant escalation - Dennis picks the most absurd example possible but Frank enables it and Mac has already prepared for it

dialogue.speaker: Dennis
dialogue.line: I mean, what, what if I said I wanted to become a man cheetah right now, what would you do?

dialogue.speaker: Frank
dialogue.line: I'd go get the spots.

dialogue.speaker: Mac
dialogue.line: He's got spots!

jokes.text: I'm storming out! / What? Don't copy me! I'm the one who's angry. Goddamn it! You don't storm out on me. I storm out on you. I'm the one who storms out!
jokes.setup: Dennis declares he's storming out
jokes.punchline: Mac tries to storm out too, leading to a fight over who gets to storm out
jokes.explanation: Perfect absurd escalation where they compete over dramatic exits, very character-specific

dialogue.speaker: Dennis
dialogue.line: I'm storming out!

dialogue.speaker: Mac
dialogue.line: What? Don't copy me! I'm the one who's angry. Goddamn it! You don't storm out on me. I storm out on you. I'm the one who storms out!

jokes.text: So, I'm not gonna storm out, but I am gonna leave. I'm gonna... leave casually.
jokes.setup: Dennis realizes the absurdity of the storming out fight
jokes.punchline: Tries to maintain dignity by leaving 'casually' but still announces it
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Dennis trying to salvage his dignity while still being petty

dialogue.speaker: Dennis
dialogue.line: So, I'm not gonna storm out, but I am gonna leave. I'm gonna... leave casually.

jokes.text: Like, I kind of feel compelled to call you a bird right now and throw my glass of water in your face.
jokes.setup: Dennis is trying to be nice to Dee without the gang around
jokes.punchline: Admits he feels compelled to do the exact mean things he usually does
jokes.explanation: Smart meta-humor about their usual dynamic, shows self-awareness

dialogue.speaker: Dennis
dialogue.line: Like, I kind of feel compelled to call you a bird right now and throw my glass of water in your face.

jokes.text: Pretend that I had to go to the bathroom and then I was gonna sneak out the window
jokes.setup: Dee admits what she was planning to do
jokes.punchline: Her escape plan is pathetically juvenile
jokes.explanation: Shows how childish and cowardly Dee can be when not performing cruelty

dialogue.speaker: Dee
dialogue.line: Pretend that I had to go to the bathroom and then I was gonna sneak out the window

jokes.text: Uh, let's see. I'd like to order my food in a German accent.
jokes.setup: Dee asks what Dennis wants to do without the gang's influence
jokes.punchline: His idea of authentic self-expression is ordering food in a German accent
jokes.explanation: Shows Dennis's idea of being 'authentic' is still performative and weird

dialogue.speaker: Dennis
dialogue.line: Uh, let's see. I'd like to order my food in a German accent.

jokes.text: I put all these expectations on myself to be funny all the time. But that's not fair. It's too much pressure for me. / Yeah, right. No, I don't think anyone expects that at all.
jokes.setup: Dennis explains why he wants to do a German accent
jokes.punchline: Dee immediately deflates his delusion that people expect him to be funny
jokes.explanation: Perfect sibling dynamic - Dee cuts down Dennis's self-important delusion with casual cruelty

dialogue.speaker: Dennis
dialogue.line: I put all these expectations on myself to be funny all the time. But that's not fair. It's too much pressure for me.

dialogue.speaker: Dee
dialogue.line: Yeah, right. No, I don't think anyone expects that at all.

jokes.text: I think I want to get the beak on the side? Frank's always making me eat it first and frankly I'd rather it last if I'm gonna have to eat it all...
jokes.setup: Dee says what she wants to order
jokes.punchline: Casually mentions Frank forces her to eat bird parts, treating it as normal
jokes.explanation: Brilliant Dee-as-bird joke that she treats as totally normal abuse from Frank

dialogue.speaker: Dee
dialogue.line: I think I want to get the beak on the side? Frank's always making me eat it first and frankly I'd rather it last if I'm gonna have to eat it all...

jokes.text: Ja, vell, uh, I vould like ze... I'm sorry. I would like a chicken sandwich, please.
jokes.setup: Dennis starts ordering in his German accent
jokes.punchline: Immediately gives up and apologizes, showing his lack of confidence
jokes.explanation: Shows Dennis can't even commit to his weird impulses

dialogue.speaker: Dennis
dialogue.line: Ja, vell, uh, I vould like ze... I'm sorry. I would like a chicken sandwich, please.

jokes.text: Beak! / No! / No.
jokes.setup: Waitress asks Dee what she wants
jokes.punchline: Dee blurts out 'Beak!' and Dennis has to stop her
jokes.explanation: Perfect callback showing how ingrained their weird habits are, great economy

dialogue.speaker: Dee
dialogue.line: Beak!

dialogue.speaker: Dennis
dialogue.line: No! / No.

jokes.text: This is not just a car. This is a lifestyle. This is an Eddie Bauer edition. Tortoise-shell interior, super-charged twin cam, European luxury sports utility vehicle, my friend.
jokes.setup: Dennis is trying to sell his car
jokes.punchline: Over-the-top sales pitch treating a car like a life philosophy
jokes.explanation: Classic Dennis grandiosity applied to car sales

dialogue.speaker: Dennis
dialogue.line: This is not just a car. This is a lifestyle. This is an Eddie Bauer edition. Tortoise-shell interior, super-charged twin cam, European luxury sports utility vehicle, my friend.

jokes.text: I used it to traverse the plains the Serengeti of my life.
jokes.setup: Dennis continues his grandiose car sales pitch
jokes.punchline: Compares his life to the Serengeti in the most pretentious way possible
jokes.explanation: Peak Dennis pretention - turning car ownership into poetic metaphor

dialogue.speaker: Dennis
dialogue.line: I used it to traverse the plains the Serengeti of my life.

jokes.text: I'm sorry. Are you talking about the car in the river?
jokes.setup: Dennis has been giving grandiose sales pitch about his amazing car
jokes.punchline: Bob's simple, confused question reveals the car is in a river
jokes.explanation: Perfect understated reaction that deflates Dennis's entire pitch

dialogue.speaker: Bob
dialogue.line: I'm sorry. Are you talking about the car in the river?

jokes.text: Yeah. Well, it's an amphibious exploring vehicle, so...
jokes.setup: Bob points out the car is in the river
jokes.punchline: Dennis doubles down with fake confidence about it being amphibious
jokes.explanation: Dennis's inability to admit fault, tries to make the disaster sound intentional

dialogue.speaker: Dennis
dialogue.line: Yeah. Well, it's an amphibious exploring vehicle, so...

jokes.text: No, no! What fun would that be, right? That's not my job. That's your job. I don't want to take that from you. You're gonna love it.
jokes.setup: Bob asks if Dennis can pull the car out of the river
jokes.punchline: Dennis frames having to retrieve a submerged car as a fun gift to the buyer
jokes.explanation: Brilliant spin where Dennis makes a massive inconvenience sound like a generous gesture

dialogue.speaker: Dennis
dialogue.line: No, no! What fun would that be, right? That's not my job. That's your job. I don't want to take that from you. You're gonna love it.

jokes.text: Hey, I'm Mac, former party boy. Looking to get back into the party-boy scene. You want me to milk your adrenal gland? You'll go berserk.
jokes.setup: Mac is trying to pick up women at a club
jokes.punchline: His pickup line is bizarre pseudo-medical nonsense delivered with confidence
jokes.explanation: Perfect Mac - combines his fitness obsession with complete social ineptitude in one line

dialogue.speaker: Mac
dialogue.line: Hey, I'm Mac, former party boy. Looking to get back into the party-boy scene. You want me to milk your adrenal gland? You'll go berserk.

jokes.text: Beat it, Frank. You're acting like it's 2006.
jokes.setup: Frank is trying to connect with Mac at the club
jokes.punchline: Mac dismisses Frank with a weirdly specific year reference
jokes.explanation: Random specific year makes it funnier, implies Frank's coolness expired at a specific time

dialogue.speaker: Mac
dialogue.line: Beat it, Frank. You're acting like it's 2006.

jokes.text: That's crazy. Black people don't do stuff that's not cool.
jokes.setup: They're wondering why Def Poetry isn't popular anymore
jokes.punchline: Charlie's bizarre logic about Black cultural authority
jokes.explanation: Classic Charlie weird logic, though treads close to problematic territory

dialogue.speaker: Charlie
dialogue.line: That's crazy. Black people don't do stuff that's not cool.

jokes.text: Arrogance, vanity-- all over. He's underwater, like a Range Rover.
jokes.setup: Charlie is doing Def Poetry about Dennis
jokes.punchline: Bad rhyme that references Dennis's car being underwater
jokes.explanation: Deliberately bad poetry that works as character comedy

dialogue.speaker: Charlie
dialogue.line: Arrogance, vanity-- all over. He's underwater, like a Range Rover.

jokes.text: Zeus, poops and shoes? Guys, you suck at Def Poetry.
jokes.setup: Charlie and Dee have been doing terrible poetry
jokes.punchline: Dennis's harsh but accurate critique of their rhyme scheme
jokes.explanation: Good payoff to the bad poetry, Dennis as the voice of harsh reality

dialogue.speaker: Dennis
dialogue.line: Zeus, poops and shoes? Guys, you suck at Def Poetry.

jokes.text: No, no, she's called Dusty because she's really into angel dust. / I am super into angel dust.
jokes.setup: Mac introduces his new girlfriend Dusty
jokes.punchline: Her name comes from drug addiction, which she cheerfully confirms
jokes.explanation: Dark humor about drug addiction treated casually, fits show's tone

dialogue.speaker: Mac
dialogue.line: No, no, she's called Dusty because she's really into angel dust.

dialogue.speaker: Dusty
dialogue.line: I am super into angel dust.

jokes.text: Does she have a dick?
jokes.setup: Mac's being defensive about his new girlfriend
jokes.punchline: Frank's blunt, inappropriate question
jokes.explanation: Crude but fits Frank's character, setup for Mac's later reveal

dialogue.speaker: Frank
dialogue.line: Does she have a dick?

jokes.text: Oh, well, the last few years, I've been settling for fives, you know, and I've been giving you the tens, okay? But then I realized, you know what, I can be a ten guy, too.
jokes.setup: Mac explains his relationship revelation
jokes.punchline: Claims he's been generously giving away 'tens' to his friends like charity
jokes.explanation: Classic Mac delusion about his sexual generosity and desirability

dialogue.speaker: Mac
dialogue.line: Oh, well, the last few years, I've been settling for fives, you know, and I've been giving you the tens, okay? But then I realized, you know what, I can be a ten guy, too.

jokes.text: Anger... bubbling like a pot. Gettin' hot. Gonna explode like a bomb in a toad.
jokes.setup: Dennis getting angry and trying def poetry
jokes.punchline: Terrible forced rhyme about bombs in toads
jokes.explanation: Intentionally bad poetry that shows Dennis trying and failing at others' activities

dialogue.speaker: Dennis
dialogue.line: Anger... bubbling like a pot. Gettin' hot. Gonna explode like a bomb in a toad.

jokes.text: There's a Jew trying to buy us out of everything. You want to get him back?
jokes.setup: Frank approaching the new bar owners
jokes.punchline: Casual antisemitism as business strategy
jokes.explanation: Weak joke that relies on casual bigotry without clever subversion - shows Frank's awfulness but not cleverly

dialogue.speaker: Frank
dialogue.line: There's a Jew trying to buy us out of everything. You want to get him back?

jokes.text: You're Charlie. / No, you're too tall. / By any chance, do you have homosexual tendencies? You could be Dee, but you're smaller. You got a better body, I think.
jokes.setup: Frank is confused about who these new bar owners are
jokes.punchline: Frank tries to figure out which gang member each person could be, with inappropriate criteria
jokes.explanation: Frank's confusion creates uncomfortable comedy as he tries to map strangers onto his gang

dialogue.speaker: Frank
dialogue.line: You're Charlie. / No, you're too tall. / By any chance, do you have homosexual tendencies? You could be Dee, but you're smaller. You got a better body, I think.

jokes.text: Our thinking: Clear. Our rhymes: No fear. Our words, our passion that flows like beer. Misery! Chains. Thunder. Rain. We're chasing the dragon. We're chasing our pain.
jokes.setup: Charlie and Dee performing their def poetry
jokes.punchline: Overly dramatic bad poetry with forced rhymes
jokes.explanation: Intentionally bad poetry performance - works as character moment but not particularly memorable

dialogue.line: Our thinking: Clear. Our rhymes: No fear. Our words, our passion that flows like beer. Misery! Chains. Thunder. Rain. We're chasing the dragon. We're chasing our pain.

jokes.text: Yeah. These are all underage kids-- tweens and such. I never got to do that scheme.
jokes.setup: Someone asks why Frank brought all these people
jokes.punchline: Casually admits to bringing underage kids to a bar as part of a scheme
jokes.explanation: Dark humor about illegal activity treated matter-of-factly by Frank

dialogue.speaker: Frank
dialogue.line: Yeah. These are all underage kids-- tweens and such. I never got to do that scheme.

jokes.text: Starter car. / Starter car?! / I have contained my rage for as long as possible, but I shall unleash my fury upon you like the crossing of a thousand waves! Begone, vile man! Begone from me! A starter car?! This car is a finisher car! A transporter of gods! The golden god! I am untethered and my rage knows no bounds!
jokes.setup: Bob calls Dennis's Range Rover a 'starter car'
jokes.punchline: Dennis erupts in an epic, over-the-top rage about the insult to his car
jokes.explanation: Legendary Dennis meltdown - perfect escalation from calm to insane rage, quotable lines, pure character distillation. This is what 8+ craft looks like.

dialogue.speaker: Bob
dialogue.line: Starter car.

dialogue.speaker: Dennis
dialogue.line: Starter car?! I have contained my rage for as long as possible, but I shall unleash my fury upon you like the crossing of a thousand waves! Begone, vile man! Begone from me! A starter car?! This car is a finisher car! A transporter of gods! The golden god! I am untethered and my rage knows no bounds!

jokes.text: Good starter car. - Starter car.
jokes.setup: New buyer examining Dennis's submerged car
jokes.punchline: Dennis's horrified repetition of 'starter car'
jokes.explanation: Perfect setup for Dennis's trigger - economical callback that builds to his explosion

dialogue.speaker: Buyer
dialogue.line: Good starter car.

dialogue.speaker: Dennis
dialogue.line: Starter car.

jokes.text: Have you been pretending to bang this chick the entire time? / No. / He can't even get it up with me.
jokes.setup: Mac is caught fully clothed after claiming to have sex
jokes.punchline: Dusty reveals Mac's complete sexual failure
jokes.explanation: Perfect deflation of Mac's false bravado about being a 'ten guy'

dialogue.speaker: Dennis
dialogue.line: Have you been pretending to bang this chick the entire time?

dialogue.speaker: Mac
dialogue.line: No.

dialogue.speaker: Dusty
dialogue.line: He can't even get it up with me.

jokes.text: He can't even get it up with me.
jokes.setup: Mac's sexual pretending exposed
jokes.punchline: Dusty bluntly reveals Mac's erectile dysfunction
jokes.explanation: Brutal escalation that further humiliates Mac - economical and effective cringe

dialogue.speaker: Dusty
dialogue.line: He can't even get it up with me.

jokes.text: Can I get my angel dust now?
jokes.setup: Mac's sexual failure has been exposed
jokes.punchline: Dusty's practical request after the awkward revelation
jokes.explanation: Dark humor capping the Mac humiliation - her priorities are clear

dialogue.speaker: Dusty
dialogue.line: Can I get my angel dust now?
